sources.sourceType: official artist website and press kit
sources.evidence: The official Bandzoogle press site identifies laurapanic as a three-piece noisegaze rock band from Newcastle, Australia and directly cross-links the Bandcamp, Facebook, Instagram and YouTube destinations used in this edition.
The official artist website directs listeners to this self-titled album as its primary album purchase destination.
The official artist website cross-links this Bandcamp profile, which identifies laurapanic and describes the music as noise and tension.
The official Bandcamp profile exposes an active merchandise catalogue; recent artist social results also advertise the current record and shirt.
The exact Instagram destination is cross-linked by both the official artist website and official YouTube channel.
The official artist website cross-links this exact LauraPanic page, which publishes the same releases and live activity.
The channel identifies laurapanic as a heavy noisegaze band from Mulubinba/Newcastle, Australia and links back to the verified artist Instagram.
The official channel’s Videos page showed denial [visualiser] at 320 views, ahead of the full live set at The Lass (221), the Denial demo (164) and Hellnation V full set (154).
Triple J Unearthed identifies laurapanic as a Newcastle NoWave/heavy noise rock band and lists Rock, Experimental and Punk alongside the featured track Denial.
No Spotify profile was linked by the official website, Bandcamp or YouTube channel and no exact identity-verified Spotify result was found; the button is intentionally omitted.
